title: ritchiea jansii
intro: Ritchiea jansii, also known as the 'Dhup' plant, is a tropical evergreen tree native to the Western Ghats of India. It has been used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The extracts from Ritchiea jansii have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, skin infections, and respiratory issues, providing benefits such as reduced inflammation and boosted immunity. The plant is often used in herbal preparations, including decoctions, infusions, and ointments, which are prepared by boiling the leaves or roots in water or mixing with other herbs to create remedies.
